channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Trump’s action against Iran is yet another wobble for government debt, warns UBS
item.description: "President Trump indicated attacks could go on for four or five weeks, and there are already reports of a need to urgently replenish weapons stockpile...

item.title: How one CEO’s counter-cultural movement became Yondr
item.description: Hello and welcome to Modern CEO ! I’m Stephanie Mehta, CEO and chief content officer of Mansueto Ventures. Each week this newsletter explores inclusiv...

item.title: Graham Dugoni Wanted to Launch a Countercultural Movement. So He Started a Company
item.description: A simple idea for a return to analog sparked a fast-growing company with A-list celebrities as partners.

item.title: I’m Afraid My Star Employee Is Going to Quit
item.description: I don’t want to lose him, but he’s upset at missing out on a promotion.

item.title: Arnold Schwarzenegger’s Definition of Success Has Changed. It’s a Lesson for the Rest of Us
item.description: Both the movie star-turned politician and psychological research say that changing your definition of success isn’t giving up. It’s growing up.

item.title: Why Sequoia’s Alfred Lin isn’t worried about the SaaS-pocalypse
item.description: Despite fear-mongering about an AI-spurred economic crisis, Lin said that software remains a verdant area to invest.

item.title: U.S. war with Iran forces CEOs to prepare for the worst—from rising energy prices to cyber attacks
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: Science Says Just 3 Minutes a Day is All It Takes to Feel Happier
item.description: Research shows the effect matters, but the cause for the effect is just as important.

item.title: How trendy ‘whole-body’ scans can miss this serious illness
item.description: The rise of full-body MRI scans has been framed as a victory for consumer empowerment. Skip the referrals. Skip the waiting. Pay out of pocket and fin...

item.title: Stocks enter global selloff but some on Wall Street are looking for assets that respond well to war
item.description: Wells Fargo’s Ohsung Kwon argued that traders should buy the dip.

item.title: This crypto ring certifies your digital self with real-life handshakes
item.description: Reality is melting away before our eyes. Identity spoofing against older adults alone grew by 8x between 2020 and 2024, driven in part by convincing A...

item.title: European markets slide as Iran ‘war trade’ joins the global AI ‘scare trade’
item.description: Opening this morning for the first working day since the military attacks at the weekend, European stock markets slumped, and the price of oil and gas...

item.title: The North Face’s former CEO just bet his reputation on this tiny startup
item.description: Over the last decade, as consumers became more aware of climate change, the fashion industry suddenly scrambled to curb its enormous environmental foo...

item.title: This genius contraption catches water while you’re warming up your shower
item.description: Australians dramatically reduced their water usage during the Millennium Drought in the 2000s. It was one of the longest recorded droughts in Australi...

item.title: Harrison Ford Just Delivered an Emotional Intelligence Masterclass. These 6 Words Were the Best Part
item.description: After accepting a lifetime achievement award from the SAG-AFTRA, Ford gave a firsthand demonstration in how to cultivate stronger relationships—and a ...

item.title: Here's how oil can reach $120 per barrel, according to JPMorgan strategists
Dow industrials poised for over 500-point drop as crude surges on Iran conflict
Gold and silver prices are rising, and so is the U.S. dollar
The workhorse fuel of the industrial world is seeing a bigger spike than crude
Anthropic’s Claude tops App Store charts as backlash builds against OpenAI
